evals.prompt: Hey, I need to add rate limiting to our Express API. Here's the spec: any endpoint under /api/v1 should be limited to 100 requests per minute per IP. Use the express-rate-limit package. Unauthenticated requests get 20 req/min. Return 429 with a JSON body {error: 'Too many requests', retryAfter: N} when exceeded. Let's code this up.
evals.expected_output: Skill should check the current branch first. If on main/master, ask for a branch name (suggest feat/rate-limiting or similar). Should produce a written development plan with tasks broken down. Should mention persisting the plan. Should include a test strategy. Should outline a conventional commit message.

assertions.name: branch_check_performed
assertions.description: The response explicitly checks or mentions checking the current git branch before proceeding to implementation

assertions.name: plan_is_structured
assertions.description: The response produces a multi-step development plan with discrete tasks, not just jumping directly to writing code

assertions.name: plan_persistence_mentioned
assertions.description: The response references saving/persisting the plan via save-plan skill, memory, or TaskCreate

assertions.name: test_strategy_included
assertions.description: The response includes a test strategy mentioning unit or integration tests for the rate limiting feature

assertions.name: commit_message_conventional
assertions.description: The proposed commit message follows conventional commits format: type(scope): description

evals.prompt: Here's the Jira ticket BP-4521: 'User profile images uploaded via the /api/users/:id/avatar endpoint are not being resized before storage, causing slow page loads. Images should be resized to max 256x256 pixels using sharp before storing to S3. Thumbnail should be 64x64.' I'm on branch fix/BP-4521-avatar-resize. Start coding.
evals.expected_output: Skill should confirm the branch is safe (not main/master/release-*). Should produce and persist a development plan referencing BP-4521. Should mention sharp library. Commit message should follow format: fix([BP-4521]): resize avatar images before S3 upload.

assertions.name: branch_confirmed_safe
assertions.description: The response confirms that fix/BP-4521-avatar-resize is a valid, safe branch to work on

assertions.name: ticket_id_in_commit_scope
assertions.description: The proposed commit message includes [BP-4521] in the scope position, e.g. fix([BP-4521]): ...

assertions.name: plan_references_sharp
assertions.description: The development plan specifically mentions the sharp library for image resizing

assertions.name: plan_persisted
assertions.description: The response mentions saving the plan using save-plan skill, serena memory, or TaskCreate

assertions.name: skill_activation_considered
assertions.description: The response considers or mentions activating a relevant language-specific or domain skill (e.g., LSP, frontend-design)

evals.prompt: ok so im on main and i need to add dark mode support to the settings page. the toggle should persist in localStorage and apply a 'dark' class to the root element. no ticket, just do it
evals.expected_output: Skill must detect that the user is on main branch and STOP. Should ask for a branch name before doing anything else. Should suggest feat/dark-mode or similar. Should NOT start implementing until the user confirms a branch name.

assertions.name: branch_guard_fires
assertions.description: The response stops and asks for a branch name when it detects the current branch is main, before any implementation

assertions.name: no_premature_implementation
assertions.description: The response does NOT start writing dark mode implementation code before the branch situation is resolved

assertions.name: branch_prefix_suggested
assertions.description: The response suggests a branch prefix such as feat/, fix/, chore/, or migration/

assertions.name: explains_constraint
assertions.description: The response explains why it cannot proceed on main (branch safety rule), not just refusing without reason
